author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
verses: kuvera paṇḍita, ati haraṣita, dekhiyā putrera mukha kari jāta⋅karma, āchila dharma, vāḍaye manera sukha sarva sulakṣaṇa varaṇa kāñcana, vadana⋅kamala śobhā ājānulambita, bāhu salalita, jaga⋅jana⋅mano⋅lobhā nābhi sagabhīra, parama sundara, nayana kamala jini aruṇa caraṇa, nakha⋅darapaṇa, jini kata vidhu maṇi mahāpuruṣera, citta manohara, dekhiyā vismaya sabe bujhi ihā haite, jagata taribe, ei kare anubhave jata pura⋅nārī, śiśumukha heri, ānanda sāyare bhāse nā dharaye hiyā, punaḥ punaḥ giyā, nirakhaye animeṣe tāhāra mātāre, kare parihāse, kahe hena suta jāra tāra bhāgya sīmā, ke diba upamā, bhuvane ke sama tāre e teka vacana, saba nārīgaṇe, kahe gadgad bhāṣa jagata tāraṇa, bujhala kāraṇa, dāsa vaiṣṇavera āśā
en_translation:
Kuvera Paṇḍita was greatly delighted to see the face of his son. He performed the birth rituals as prescribed by religious duty, and his happiness increased manifold.

The child was endowed with all auspicious markings and a golden complexion. He had the beauty of a lotus, and His graceful arms reached down to His knees, captivating the minds of all people.

He had a deep navel, was supremely beautiful, and His eyes surpassed the beauty of lotus flowers. His feet were reddish, and His toenails were like mirrors, conquering the luster of millions of moons and jewels.

Everyone was astonished to see these charming signs of a Great Personality, and they felt that through Him, the world would be delivered.

All the women of the town, beholding the child's face, floated in an ocean of bliss. Their hearts could not contain their joy, and they went again and again to gaze at Him without blinking.

They jokingly said to His mother, "Who can describe the limit of the fortune of one who has such a son? Who in the world is equal to her?"

All the women spoke with voices choked with emotion, understanding the reason for the world's deliverance. This is the hope of Vaiṣṇava Dāsa.
